title: Banisteriopsis sellowiana
intro: Banisteriopsis sellowiana, also known as Sellow's Banisteriopsis or Yellow Banisteriopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its ability to aid in weight loss, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Banisteriopsis sellowiana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, although it is also used in the production of ayahuasca, a psychoactive brew traditionally used in shamanic rituals.
